content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

No source code changes were made — the implementation correctly benchmarked debug vs release profiles and found the hypothesis disproven. Single test: 69.65s debug vs 65.63s release (~6% improvement, still 4x over the 15s target). Full CLI suite and conformance tests were actually slower in release mode. The decision not to add [profile.test] opt-level is well-supported by data. The final tree passes all verification checks (cargo test, clippy, fmt, nix build). No bugs, safety issues, or correctness problems to report since no code was changed. The underlying performance problem (70s for a single lifecycle test) remains unresolved and likely stems from I/O, subprocess overhead, or sleep-based test timeouts rather than CPU-bound computation, which would explain why compiler optimizations don't help.

## Amendments

1. The acceptance criteria specify 'Target: single CLI lifecycle test under 15 seconds (currently 67-85s)' and 'Measurable reduction in stub-backend test execution time'. Neither criterion is met. While the hypothesis was correctly tested and disproven (release mode yields ~6% improvement on the single test but the full suite and conformance are actually slower), the performance problem persists at 69.65s — 4.6x over the 15s target. A new bead should investigate the actual bottleneck. The release-vs-debug data strongly suggests the overhead is not CPU-bound (compiler optimizations don't help), so the root cause is likely: (1) excessive sleep/timeout durations in stub backends, (2) subprocess spawning overhead, or (3) synchronization/polling intervals in the test harness. Profiling with strace/perf to identify the dominant wait source would be the right next step.
   Rationale: The opt-level hypothesis was correctly disproven, but the 70s test runtime problem remains. The benchmark data (release mode is not faster for the full suite) provides a strong signal that the bottleneck is I/O/sleep-based, not CPU. A follow-up bead should profile the actual wait sources and reduce them — likely stub backend timeouts, polling intervals, or subprocess overhead.
